details.body: Post-merge bead mutations are not persisted before the next hard reset. The success path closes the bead after PR merge in `src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:215-217`, and skip/follow-up paths mutate beads in `src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:393-404`, but the concrete port only runs `br close`/`br create` plus guarded `br sync` in `src/cli/drain.rs:230-265`. The next cycle starts with `git reset --hard origin/master` in `src/cli/drain.rs:113-116`, so those uncommitted `.beads/` changes can be discarded. Persist the final bead mutations to master, for example by syncing, committing, and pushing `.beads/` after merge or by otherwise ensuring the close/follow-up state lands before continuing.

The concrete run adapter treats any `execute_start`/`execute_resume` success as a completed run. `ProcessDrainPorts::start_run` returns `DrainRunOutcome::Completed` on any `Ok(())` in `src/cli/drain.rs:162-166`, but `execute_start` can return `Ok(())` after a paused run and merely print the paused status in `src/cli/run.rs:4256-4263`. Drain then attempts `open_pr`, where `pr_open` rejects non-completed snapshots in `src/contexts/bead_workflow/pr_open.rs:501-504`, bypassing `classify_drain_failure`. Read the final run snapshot after start/resume and map non-completed states to the appropriate `FailureObservationKind` before deciding whether to open a PR.

SIGINT is only observed after an entire bead cycle completes. The loop starts sync, bead selection, project creation, run start, PR open, and PR watch before checking `should_interrupt` at `src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:344-350`; there is no check before `sync_master_and_sanity_check` at `src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:192`. A Ctrl-C during sync, run, or PR watch can therefore continue processing work instead of returning `Interrupted{...}` promptly. Check the interrupt flag at the top of the loop and between long operations, and make the run/watch paths cancellable or interrupt-aware.

`src/cli/drain.rs:187` and `src/cli/drain.rs:210` discard the actual run failure whenever a non-completed `run.json` can be read, and `src/cli/drain.rs:509` maps every `RunStatus::Failed` snapshot to `FailureClass::DomainValidationFailure`. That means real failures such as `BackendExhausted` or cancellation/interruption are never passed to `classify_drain_failure` with their true class, so the required recovery behavior is wrong: backend exhaustion should file a follow-up and skip/continue, while an interrupted run should resume, but the drain will classify both as domain validation and abort/file the wrong bead. Preserve the failure class from the run journal/latest stage failure, or otherwise pass the original classified failure into `FailureObservation` before calling the gj74 classifier.

`src/cli/drain.rs:175` and `src/cli/drain.rs:198` race `execute_start`/`execute_resume` against a separate drain-level interrupt flag. If SIGINT arrives, `wait_until_interrupted` can win and the `execute_*` future is dropped before its own `run_with_termination_signal_waiter` cleanup path (`src/cli/run.rs:3287`) can cancel the engine, persist interrupted handoff state, remove pid files, and release the writer lease. A user interrupt can therefore strand a Running snapshot or live backend process instead of producing the required durable `Interrupted{...}` outcome. Let the existing run start/resume signal handling own cleanup, or share a cancellation path that awaits the cleanup before returning the drain outcome.

src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:236 propagates `open_pr` failures with `?`, and src/cli/drain.rs:252-270 maps all PR-open errors into `DrainError`, so local gate failures such as `PrOpenError::GateFailed` abort before the drain can build a `FailureObservation`. Similarly, src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:244-268 collapses `WatchOutcome::BlockedByCi` into `DrainOutcome::Failed` without filing a follow-up bead. The production gate/CI failure paths therefore bypass gj74's `VerificationFailureSource::{Gate,Ci}` policy even though those variants are tested. Convert PR-open gate failures and PR-watch CI failures into `FailureObservationKind::VerificationFailure` and route them through `handle_recovery_action`.

src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:277-278 discards the `FailureObservation` after classification, `handle_recovery_action` only receives `RecoveryAction` at src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:472-487, and src/cli/drain.rs:353-356 files a generic follow-up bead containing only `Recovery action: ...`. For `FileBead` recoveries this loses the actionable details: failing test names, gate name, run id, failure class, or bot details. Pass the original observation into follow-up bead creation and include those diagnostics in the created bead.

src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain_failure.rs:102-103 returns `RecoveryAction::ResumeRun { stop_first: true }` for interrupted runs, but src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:482 ignores the `stop_first` field, and src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:298-370 resumes immediately in the same cycle and fails the entire drain on the first failed resume. This does not implement the documented interrupted-run policy to stop/clean up first and resume on the next drain cycle with limited retry/skip behavior. Honor `stop_first` through the run-stop path and model resume as a next-cycle retryable state instead of immediate hard failure.

src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:215 and src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:266-270 make the pending resume path run after a fresh master reset, not on the bead's feature branch. ProcessDrainPorts checks out master in src/cli/drain.rs:153-162, while create_project_from_bead creates a feature branch but the returned branch name is discarded at src/cli/drain.rs:185-204. After a RunInterrupted queues pending_resume_bead, the next loop resets away the branch context and then calls resume_run on master, so a resumed run can lose the work tree and later fail pr open as a non-feature branch. If this is fixed by staying on the feature branch, src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:231 will still skip the bead because create_project_from_bead marks it in_progress at src/contexts/bead_workflow/create_project.rs:214. Store and restore the created branch for pending resumes, and allow the currently claimed bead status instead of only open.

src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:589-591 converts RecoveryAction::Rerun into an Abort/Failed outcome. The bead contract says Rerun is the CI-flake class owned by pr watch and should be ignored by the drain layer; this implementation stops the whole drain if that action reaches handle_recovery_action. Treat it as a drain no-op/continue case, or make it an explicit unreachable invariant before classification can return it here, instead of surfacing Failed.

`nix build` fails on the final tree in `src/adapters/br_process.rs:2858`. The test writes the fake `custom-br` script using `test_shell()` (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:2247`), whose fallback `/bin/sh` is not available in the nix sandbox, so the configured-binary health-check test reports `br` as unavailable. Fix the test to use a sandbox-available interpreter or avoid a shell script so the authoritative gate passes.

Pending resume state can be destroyed before `run resume`. `src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:393` queues a pending resume, but the next loop runs `sync_master_and_sanity_check()` first (`src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:230`). The process port implements that sync as `git reset --hard` on the current branch before switching to master (`src/cli/drain.rs:153`). If the current branch is the interrupted bead branch, uncommitted run snapshot, journal, or worktree state needed for resume can be discarded before `restore_resume_context` runs. Preserve the feature-branch state, or sync master without hard-resetting the pending resume branch.

A SIGINT that lands during a successful PR merge can leave the bead unclosed. `complete_success_path` awaits `watch_pr` at `src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:524`, checks interruption at line 525, and only then handles `WatchOutcome::Merged` at line 528. If `watch_pr` has already merged the PR when the interrupt flag is set, drain returns `Interrupted` and skips the `Landed via PR #N` close/persist step. Handle `Merged` before honoring the interrupt, or make post-merge bead closure run as idempotent cleanup.

`FailureObservationKind::AmendmentOscillationLimitReached` is only reachable in mocks, not through the real drain CLI path. The journal mapper only reads `failure_class` (`src/cli/drain.rs:753`), max-completion-round failures are emitted as unclassified `StageCommitFailed`/`unknown` with completion-round metadata (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:8231`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:8372`), and the app-error fallback defaults unclassified errors to `TransportFailure` (`src/cli/drain.rs:659`). A real final-review oscillation will therefore file a follow-up and abort instead of taking the `ForceCompleteRun` success path (`src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:432`). Amend this bead to parse the max-completion-round journal shape into `AmendmentOscillationLimitReached` and add a test using that real RunFailed shape.

`ProcessDrainPorts::resume_run` treats a completed snapshot as failure whenever `execute_resume` returns an error (`src/cli/drain.rs:281`). But `execute_resume` deliberately returns `ResumeFailed` when stale-run reconciliation discovers the run already completed (`src/cli/run.rs:4356`) or when the snapshot is already completed (`src/cli/run.rs:4299`). In a queued resume cycle, that turns an actually completed bead into a transport failure and aborts/files a follow-up instead of opening and watching the PR. Amend the current bead to classify the already-completed resume case as `DrainRunOutcome::Completed` and cover it with a pending-resume test.

Fix the master sync order in `src/cli/drain.rs:175`. `sync_master_and_sanity_check` runs `git checkout -B master origin/master` before the hard reset at `src/cli/drain.rs:181`; with any dirty tracked file, checkout can fail before the drain gets to the reset that is supposed to discard local state and sync master. Reset or force-clean the current worktree before switching branches, or use a forced checkout, and add a regression test with a dirty tracked file.
